revision.text:
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> * It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> * First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): * July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. * Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): * August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}. * November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. * November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): * May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. * September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): * September: Construction of the school building began. * November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." * A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): * May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. * September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): * September: Construction of the school building began. * November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." * A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.

'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> * It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> * First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): * July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. * Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): * August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}. * November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. * November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): * May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. * September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): * September: Construction of the school building began. * November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." * A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.
== References == <references/>

It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> * First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): * July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. * Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): * August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}. * November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. * November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): * May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. * September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): * September: Construction of the school building began. * November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." * A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.

'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).

This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

{{draft categories| [[Category:Normal schools in Japan]] [[Category:Defunct schools in Japan]] [[Category:Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Miyazaki Normal School]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Miyazaki Normal School]] [[ja:宮崎師範学校]] [[en:Miyazaki Normal School]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 6]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=draft}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学|12=draft}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市|12=draft}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|12=draft}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|12=draft}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=draft}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学|12=draft}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学|12=draft}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=draft}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]
<!--Miyazaki Normal School-->

==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=draft|12=draft}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革|12=draft|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to ""
revision.text:
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

revision.comment: 29 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宮崎師範学校 up to rev 99990476
revision.text:
'''宮崎師範学校''' （みやざきしはんがっこう） は[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1943年]] （[[昭和]]18年） に、[[宮崎県]]に設置された[[師範学校]]である。
本項は、'''宮崎県師範学校'''・'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''などの前身諸校を含めて記述する。
== 概要 == *'''宮崎県師範学校'''・'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''の統合・官立移管により設置され、男子部・女子部を置いた。 *現・宮崎県成立翌年の[[1884年]] （[[明治]]17年） に設立された （県立）'''宮崎師範学校''' を起源とする。 *第二次世界大戦後の[[学制改革]]で新制'''[[宮崎大学]]学芸学部''' （現・教育学部） の前身の一つとなった。 *同窓会は 「'''木犀会'''」 と称し、旧制・新制 （学芸学部・教育学部・教育文化学部） 合同の会である。
== 沿革 == === 旧・宮崎県立期 === ==== 仮小学講習所、宮崎学校 ==== *[[1873年]]（明治6年）10月： 旧・[[宮崎県]]、'''仮小学講習所'''を設置 （講習期間： 約1ヶ月）<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（[[2000年]]） 260頁-263頁。</ref>。 *[[1874年]]（明治7年）8月： 宮崎県、'''宮崎学校'''を設立 （[[9月2日]]開校）<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 177頁</ref>。 **中等教育および教員養成を目的とした。旧宮崎県仮庁舎 （現・[[宮崎市]]橘通3丁目付近<ref>『宮崎市史』（[[1959年]]） 629頁。市史も約50年前の編纂であり、今日の地名は定かでない。</ref>） に設置。 **初代校長： [[野村綱]]。 *[[1875年]]（明治8年） **7月： 教則を改正し、中学予科を14歳以上、師範学生を17歳以上とする。 **冬： 宮崎県、官立師範学校卒業生13名を招聘。うち10名を県内各地に派遣。県内大区の小学校等に師範学科を設置、小学校教員の講習に当たらせた。
=== 鹿児島県立期 === ==== 第一大区小学師範学講習所 ==== *[[1876年]]（明治9年） **[[8月21日]]： 旧・宮崎県、[[鹿児島県]]に吸収合併される。 **[[11月2日]]： 宮崎学校、廃校。 **[[11月4日]]： '''第一大区小学師範学講習所'''を設置 （[[1877年]]（明治10年）[[西南戦争]]で閉校）。 *[[1879年]]（明治12年）4月： 宮崎郡上別府村外39町村共立で再開 （附属小学校は現・宮崎市立宮崎小学校）。 *[[1881年]]（明治14年）9月： 竜巻で校舎転覆。小学生徒16名圧死。 *[[1885年]]（明治18年）5月： 共立師範校は再開しないまま廃止 （宮崎県師範学校開校のため）。
=== 宮崎県立期 === ==== 旧・宮崎師範学校 ==== *[[1883年]]（明治15年） **[[5月9日]]： 現・宮崎県が成立。 **9月： 宮崎県学務課、師範学校設立に関して上申<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 657頁。以下、[[1897年]]頃までの記述は同書656頁-665頁に拠る。</ref>。師範学校設置を求める[[文部省]]に対して、直近の設置は難しいが設置する意向と回答。 *[[1884年]]（明治17年） **9月： 校舎着工。 **[[11月17日]]： 「宮崎師範学校」設置認可<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 185頁</ref>。 *[[1885年]]（明治18年）[[2月28日]]： 校舎が完成し、「'''宮崎師範学校'''」が開校<ref>『官報』明治18年3月17日学事欄「○師範学校開校式（宮崎県報告）宮崎県師範学校ハ昨年九月ヨリ工事ニ着手シ本年二月落成セシヲ以テ同月廿八日午前第九時…開校式ヲ執行セリ又同日午後ヨリ校内ニ於テ理化学試験ヲ施行シテ衆庶ニ縦覧セシメタリ」。</ref>。 **師範学校教則大綱に準拠し、高等師範学科・中等師範学科および教員講習科 （現職教員の研修機関） を設置。 **[[宮崎郡]]下北方村<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 185頁では宮崎郡下北方村、『宮崎市史』（1959年） 633頁では宮崎町和知川原</ref>に所在。
==== 宮崎県尋常師範学校 ==== *[[1886年]]（明治19年）4月10日： [[師範学校令]]に準拠し「'''宮崎県尋常師範学校'''」と改称 （本科4年制）<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 187頁</ref>。 *[[1887年]]（明治20年） **4月： [[宮崎市立宮崎小学校|宮崎小学校]]を代用附属校とする。 **この年 : 女子の入学を許可。翌[[1888年]]（明治21年）、第1回卒業のみで女子の受け入れは中止。 *[[1893年]]（明治26年） **4月： [[宮崎大学教育学部附属小学校|附属小学校]]を新設 （宮崎郡大宮村、現・宮崎市花殿町、[[宮崎大学教育学部附属中学校|宮崎大学附属中]]付近）。 **この年 : 簡易科 （2年4ヶ月） を設置 （[[1896年]]（明治29年）7月、第2回卒業で廃止）。 *[[1895年]]（明治28年） **9月： 講習科を改組、2年制に。 **11月： 予備科 （6ヶ月） を設置。
==== 宮崎県師範学校 ==== *[[1898年]]（明治31年）4月： [[師範教育令]]に準拠し「'''宮崎県師範学校'''」と改称<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 946頁。以下、[[1915年]]頃までの記述は同書945頁-953頁に拠る。</ref>。 *[[1899年]]（明治32年）2月： 予備科を1年制に延長。予備科は[[1904年]]（明治37年）廃止。[[1905年]]（明治38年） - [[1921年]]（大正10年）3月は私立向陽学舎が代役を担った。 *[[1907年]]（明治40年）3月： 女子部を開設<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 203頁</ref>。 *[[1908年]]（明治41年）： 師範学校規程に準拠し、本科第一部 （男女とも、4年制）・本科第二部 （男子のみ、1年制、[[旧制中学校|中学校]]卒対象） を設置。 *[[1913年]]（大正2年）： 通学生を認める。 *[[1915年]]（大正4年）： 大宮村立大宮尋常高等小学校 （現・[[宮崎市立大宮小学校]]） を女子部代用附属小学校とする。 *[[1922年]]（大正11年）3月： 女子部に本科第二部を設置 （[[高等女学校|高女]]卒対象）。男子本科第二部の募集を中止<ref>以下、[[1928年]]頃までの記述は、『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 1205頁-1208頁に拠る。</ref>。 *[[1925年]]（大正14年）4月： 本科第一部を5年制に変更 （2年制[[高等小学校|高小]]卒対象）。 *[[1926年]]（大正15年）4月1日： 女子部を分離し「'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''」を開校。 **宮崎市花殿町 （現・船塚1丁目、[[宮崎公立大学]]校地）。 **宮崎県師範学校は男子校となった。同月、専攻科 （1年制） を設置。 *[[1931年]]（昭和6年）3月： 本科第二部を2年制に延長<ref>以下、『宮崎市史 : 続編（上）』（[[1978年]]） 775頁-776頁に拠る。</ref>。 *[[1940年]]（昭和15年）3月： 本科第二部特別学級 「大陸科」 を設置 （[[1944年]]、本科に統合）。 *[[1941年]]（昭和16年）4月 : [[国民学校令]]により、附属小学校を附属[[国民学校]]に改称。
==== 宮崎県女子師範学校 ==== *[[1907年]]（明治40年）3月： 宮崎県師範学校に女子部を開設。 *[[1922年]]（大正11年）3月： 女子部に本科第二部を設置 （[[高等女学校|高女]]卒対象）。 *[[1926年]]（大正15年） **1月： 宮崎県会、宮崎県師範学校女子部の廃止・女子師範学校設置を議決。 **[[2月23日]]： 宮崎県女子師範学校設置認可。 **4月1日： 県師範学校女子部を廃止し、「'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''」が開校。 ***宮崎市花殿町 （現・船塚1丁目、[[宮崎公立大学]]校地）。 ***本科第一部 （5年制）・本科第二部 （2年制） を設置。第四宮崎小学校 （現・大宮小学校） を代用附属校に。 **5月： 専攻科 （1年制） を設置。 *[[1928年]]（昭和3年）[[12月15日]]： 女子師範学校の[[都城市|都城]]移転問題に絡む県会議場襲撃事件発生。 **宮崎県知事 （[[立憲政友会|政友会]]、移転推進） と宮崎市長 （[[政友本党]]床次派、移転反対） との対立が原因とされる。市消防組が議場に放水、群衆が議場になだれ込んだ<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 1207頁。『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 2』（2000年） 10頁-12頁。</ref>。 *[[1930年]]（昭和5年）12月 : 女子師範の宮崎存置、[[宮崎県立都城商業高等学校|都城商業学校]]への工科設置で決着。 *[[1935年]]（昭和10年） **3月： 校舎新築。 **4月： 附属小学校を開設。第四宮崎小学校は代用附属から解かれる。 **11月13日-14日： [[昭和天皇|天皇]]行幸<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=77 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}</ref>。 *1941年（昭和16年）4月 : 国民学校令により、附属小学校を附属国民学校に改称。
=== 官立期 === ==== 宮崎師範学校 ==== *[[1943年]]（昭和18年）4月1日： 宮崎県師範学校・宮崎県女子師範学校を統合し、官立「'''宮崎師範学校'''」設置。 **旧宮崎県師範学校校舎に'''男子部'''、旧宮崎県女子師範学校校舎に'''女子部'''を設置。 **本科 （3年制。[[旧制中等教育学校|中等学校]]卒対象）・予科 （2年制。高等小学校卒対象） を設置。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年） **5月11日： 空襲で校舎倒壊。男子部予科生徒6名死亡。 **6月6日： 本科生、動員先の名古屋で7名爆死。 **8月12日： 宮崎大空襲で男子部・女子部ともに全焼。 **9月17日： [[枕崎台風]]で焼け残り寮舎倒壊。 **11月： 仮校舎で授業再開。男子部は[[川南町|川南村]]旧軍用施設、女子部は[[宮崎神宮]]徴古館を使用。 *[[1947年]]（昭和22年） **2月： [[宮崎大学教育学部附属小学校|附属小学校]]改築。 **4月： [[学制改革]]により、附属国民学校を附属小学校と[[宮崎大学教育学部附属中学校|附属中学校]]（[[新制中学校]]）に改組。 **9月： 川南から復帰。 *[[1949年]]（昭和24年）[[5月31日]]： 新制「'''宮崎大学'''」が発足。 **宮崎師範学校は[[宮崎青年師範学校]]と共に学芸学部の母体として包括された。在校生は「'''宮崎大学宮崎師範学校'''」の生徒となる。 *[[1951年]]（昭和26年）3月： 宮崎大学宮崎師範学校 （旧制）、廃止。
== 歴代校長 == {{節スタブ}}
;宮崎県師範学校（前身諸校を含む） *太田忠恕：1885年2月 - 1886年6月 *遠藤正：1886年6月 - 1908年5月1日 *[[東基吉]]：1908年5月1日<ref>『官報』第7452号、明治41年5月2日。</ref> - 10月14日<ref>『宮崎県大百科事典』（[[1983年]]） 878頁-879頁は、第3代校長のみ名前を挙げていない。</ref> *本田嘉種：1908年10月14日 - 1910年4月23日 *児玉鑑三：1910年4月23日 - 1915年4月27日<ref>『宮崎県大百科事典』（1983年） 878頁-879頁に挙げられている校長は第5代まで。</ref> *森岡格：1915年4月27日 -
;宮崎県女子師範学校 *山田恒治：1926年4月 - *井東豊彦：1930年9月 - *上地亀義 ：1937年3月 - ? <ref>『宮崎県大百科事典』（1983年） 879頁に挙げられている校長は第3代まで。</ref>
;官立宮崎師範学校 *長谷川亀太郎：1943年4月1日<ref>『官報』第4865号、昭和18年4月2日。</ref> - 1945年11月24日<ref name=kp5664>『官報』第5664号、昭和20年11月28日。</ref> *中村友平：1945年11月24日<ref name=kp5664/> - *片岡一亀:
== 校地の変遷と継承 == ;宮崎師範学校男子部 前身の宮崎県師範学校から引き継いだ現・宮崎市花殿町の校地を使用した。第二次世界大戦末期、1945年の空襲で校舎を焼失し、戦後の授業開始時は川南村 （現・川南町） の軍施設を使用したが、1947年9月に宮崎市に復帰した。旧男子部校地は、現在は[[宮崎大学教育学部附属中学校]]・[[宮崎大学教育学部附属小学校]]の校地となっている。
;宮崎師範学校女子部 前身の宮崎県女子師範学校から引き継いだ現・宮崎市船塚1丁目の校地を使用した。1945年の空襲で校舎を焼失し、戦後の授業開始時は宮崎神宮徴古館を借用した。旧女子部校地は後身の宮崎大学学芸学部 （[[1966年]]から教育学部） の校地として引き継がれ、同学部が[[1988年]]9月に現在の木花キャンパスに統合移転するまで使用された。旧女子部校地は、現在は[[宮崎公立大学]]の校地となっている。
== 著名な出身者 == {{See|宮崎大学の人物一覧#旧制学校時代の出身者}}
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 関連書籍 == *宮崎県（編） 『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』 宮崎県、[[2000年]]5月、260頁-263頁・656頁-665頁・945頁-953頁・1205頁-1208頁。 *宮崎県（編） 『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 2』 宮崎県、2000年5月、10頁-12頁。 *宮崎県（編） 『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』 宮崎県、2000年5月。 *宮崎市史編纂委員会（編） 『宮崎市史』 宮崎市、[[1959年]]3月、632頁-634頁。 *宮崎市史編纂委員会（編） 『宮崎市史 : 続編（上）』 宮崎市、[[1978年]]3月、775頁-776頁。 *宮崎日日新聞社・宮崎県大百科事典刊行委員会（編） 『宮崎県大百科事典』 宮崎日日新聞社、[[1983年]]、「宮崎県師範学校」・「宮崎県女子師範学校」 の項。
== 関連項目 == *[[宮崎高等農林学校]]・[[宮崎青年師範学校]]・[[宮崎県工業専門学校 (旧制)]] - 新制[[宮崎大学]]の旧制前身校 *[[師範学校]] *[[旧制専門学校]] *[[学制改革]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ 宮崎大学教育学部] - 後身校 **[http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/faculty/history.html 沿革] *[http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ 木犀会] - 同窓会 *[https://www.the-miyanichi.co.jp/contents/index.php?itemid=2379&catid=74 宮崎師範学校予科生の慰霊碑建立] - 宮崎日日新聞 2007年10月8日付
{{日本の師範学校}} {{School-stub|pref=宮崎県}} {{デフォルトソート:みやさきしはん}} [[Category:日本の師範学校|みやさき]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧制教育機関|しはん]] [[Category:宮崎大学|旧しはん]] [[Category:宮崎市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[宮崎師範学校]] to [[Miyazaki Normal School]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''宮崎師範学校''' （みやざきしはんがっこう） は[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1943年]] （[[昭和]]18年） に、[[宮崎県]]に設置された[[師範学校]]である。
本項は、'''宮崎県師範学校'''・'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''などの前身諸校を含めて記述する。
== 概要 == *'''宮崎県師範学校'''・'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''の統合・官立移管により設置され、男子部・女子部を置いた。 *現・宮崎県成立翌年の[[1884年]] （[[明治]]17年） に設立された （県立）'''宮崎師範学校''' を起源とする。 *第二次世界大戦後の[[学制改革]]で新制'''[[宮崎大学]]学芸学部''' （現・教育学部） の前身の一つとなった。 *同窓会は 「'''木犀会'''」 と称し、旧制・新制 （学芸学部・教育学部・教育文化学部） 合同の会である。
== 沿革 == === 旧・宮崎県立期 === ==== 仮小学講習所、宮崎学校 ==== *[[1873年]]（明治6年）10月： 旧・[[宮崎県]]、'''仮小学講習所'''を設置 （講習期間： 約1ヶ月）<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（[[2000年]]） 260頁-263頁。</ref>。 *[[1874年]]（明治7年）8月： 宮崎県、'''宮崎学校'''を設立 （[[9月2日]]開校）<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 177頁</ref>。 **中等教育および教員養成を目的とした。旧宮崎県仮庁舎 （現・[[宮崎市]]橘通3丁目付近<ref>『宮崎市史』（[[1959年]]） 629頁。市史も約50年前の編纂であり、今日の地名は定かでない。</ref>） に設置。 **初代校長： [[野村綱]]。 *[[1875年]]（明治8年） **7月： 教則を改正し、中学予科を14歳以上、師範学生を17歳以上とする。 **冬： 宮崎県、官立師範学校卒業生13名を招聘。うち10名を県内各地に派遣。県内大区の小学校等に師範学科を設置、小学校教員の講習に当たらせた。
=== 鹿児島県立期 === ==== 第一大区小学師範学講習所 ==== *[[1876年]]（明治9年） **[[8月21日]]： 旧・宮崎県、[[鹿児島県]]に吸収合併される。 **[[11月2日]]： 宮崎学校、廃校。 **[[11月4日]]： '''第一大区小学師範学講習所'''を設置 （[[1877年]]（明治10年）[[西南戦争]]で閉校）。 *[[1879年]]（明治12年）4月： 宮崎郡上別府村外39町村共立で再開 （附属小学校は現・宮崎市立宮崎小学校）。 *[[1881年]]（明治14年）9月： 竜巻で校舎転覆。小学生徒16名圧死。 *[[1885年]]（明治18年）5月： 共立師範校は再開しないまま廃止 （宮崎県師範学校開校のため）。
=== 宮崎県立期 === ==== 旧・宮崎師範学校 ==== *[[1883年]]（明治15年） **[[5月9日]]： 現・宮崎県が成立。 **9月： 宮崎県学務課、師範学校設立に関して上申<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 657頁。以下、[[1897年]]頃までの記述は同書656頁-665頁に拠る。</ref>。師範学校設置を求める[[文部省]]に対して、直近の設置は難しいが設置する意向と回答。 *[[1884年]]（明治17年） **9月： 校舎着工。 **[[11月17日]]： 「宮崎師範学校」設置認可<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 185頁</ref>。 *[[1885年]]（明治18年）[[2月28日]]： 校舎が完成し、「'''宮崎師範学校'''」が開校<ref>『官報』明治18年3月17日学事欄「○師範学校開校式（宮崎県報告）宮崎県師範学校ハ昨年九月ヨリ工事ニ着手シ本年二月落成セシヲ以テ同月廿八日午前第九時…開校式ヲ執行セリ又同日午後ヨリ校内ニ於テ理化学試験ヲ施行シテ衆庶ニ縦覧セシメタリ」。</ref>。 **師範学校教則大綱に準拠し、高等師範学科・中等師範学科および教員講習科 （現職教員の研修機関） を設置。 **[[宮崎郡]]下北方村<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 185頁では宮崎郡下北方村、『宮崎市史』（1959年） 633頁では宮崎町和知川原</ref>に所在。
==== 宮崎県尋常師範学校 ==== *[[1886年]]（明治19年）4月10日： [[師範学校令]]に準拠し「'''宮崎県尋常師範学校'''」と改称 （本科4年制）<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 187頁</ref>。 *[[1887年]]（明治20年） **4月： [[宮崎市立宮崎小学校|宮崎小学校]]を代用附属校とする。 **この年 : 女子の入学を許可。翌[[1888年]]（明治21年）、第1回卒業のみで女子の受け入れは中止。 *[[1893年]]（明治26年） **4月： [[宮崎大学教育学部附属小学校|附属小学校]]を新設 （宮崎郡大宮村、現・宮崎市花殿町、[[宮崎大学教育学部附属中学校|宮崎大学附属中]]付近）。 **この年 : 簡易科 （2年4ヶ月） を設置 （[[1896年]]（明治29年）7月、第2回卒業で廃止）。 *[[1895年]]（明治28年） **9月： 講習科を改組、2年制に。 **11月： 予備科 （6ヶ月） を設置。
==== 宮崎県師範学校 ==== *[[1898年]]（明治31年）4月： [[師範教育令]]に準拠し「'''宮崎県師範学校'''」と改称<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 946頁。以下、[[1915年]]頃までの記述は同書945頁-953頁に拠る。</ref>。 *[[1899年]]（明治32年）2月： 予備科を1年制に延長。予備科は[[1904年]]（明治37年）廃止。[[1905年]]（明治38年） - [[1921年]]（大正10年）3月は私立向陽学舎が代役を担った。 *[[1907年]]（明治40年）3月： 女子部を開設<ref>『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』（2000年） 203頁</ref>。 *[[1908年]]（明治41年）： 師範学校規程に準拠し、本科第一部 （男女とも、4年制）・本科第二部 （男子のみ、1年制、[[旧制中学校|中学校]]卒対象） を設置。 *[[1913年]]（大正2年）： 通学生を認める。 *[[1915年]]（大正4年）： 大宮村立大宮尋常高等小学校 （現・[[宮崎市立大宮小学校]]） を女子部代用附属小学校とする。 *[[1922年]]（大正11年）3月： 女子部に本科第二部を設置 （[[高等女学校|高女]]卒対象）。男子本科第二部の募集を中止<ref>以下、[[1928年]]頃までの記述は、『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 1205頁-1208頁に拠る。</ref>。 *[[1925年]]（大正14年）4月： 本科第一部を5年制に変更 （2年制[[高等小学校|高小]]卒対象）。 *[[1926年]]（大正15年）4月1日： 女子部を分離し「'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''」を開校。 **宮崎市花殿町 （現・船塚1丁目、[[宮崎公立大学]]校地）。 **宮崎県師範学校は男子校となった。同月、専攻科 （1年制） を設置。 *[[1931年]]（昭和6年）3月： 本科第二部を2年制に延長<ref>以下、『宮崎市史 : 続編（上）』（[[1978年]]） 775頁-776頁に拠る。</ref>。 *[[1940年]]（昭和15年）3月： 本科第二部特別学級 「大陸科」 を設置 （[[1944年]]、本科に統合）。 *[[1941年]]（昭和16年）4月 : [[国民学校令]]により、附属小学校を附属[[国民学校]]に改称。
==== 宮崎県女子師範学校 ==== *[[1907年]]（明治40年）3月： 宮崎県師範学校に女子部を開設。 *[[1922年]]（大正11年）3月： 女子部に本科第二部を設置 （[[高等女学校|高女]]卒対象）。 *[[1926年]]（大正15年） **1月： 宮崎県会、宮崎県師範学校女子部の廃止・女子師範学校設置を議決。 **[[2月23日]]： 宮崎県女子師範学校設置認可。 **4月1日： 県師範学校女子部を廃止し、「'''宮崎県女子師範学校'''」が開校。 ***宮崎市花殿町 （現・船塚1丁目、[[宮崎公立大学]]校地）。 ***本科第一部 （5年制）・本科第二部 （2年制） を設置。第四宮崎小学校 （現・大宮小学校） を代用附属校に。 **5月： 専攻科 （1年制） を設置。 *[[1928年]]（昭和3年）[[12月15日]]： 女子師範学校の[[都城市|都城]]移転問題に絡む県会議場襲撃事件発生。 **宮崎県知事 （[[立憲政友会|政友会]]、移転推進） と宮崎市長 （[[政友本党]]床次派、移転反対） との対立が原因とされる。市消防組が議場に放水、群衆が議場になだれ込んだ<ref>『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』（2000年） 1207頁。『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 2』（2000年） 10頁-12頁。</ref>。 *[[1930年]]（昭和5年）12月 : 女子師範の宮崎存置、[[宮崎県立都城商業高等学校|都城商業学校]]への工科設置で決着。 *[[1935年]]（昭和10年） **3月： 校舎新築。 **4月： 附属小学校を開設。第四宮崎小学校は代用附属から解かれる。 **11月13日-14日： [[昭和天皇|天皇]]行幸<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=77 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}</ref>。 *1941年（昭和16年）4月 : 国民学校令により、附属小学校を附属国民学校に改称。
=== 官立期 === ==== 宮崎師範学校 ==== *[[1943年]]（昭和18年）4月1日： 宮崎県師範学校・宮崎県女子師範学校を統合し、官立「'''宮崎師範学校'''」設置。 **旧宮崎県師範学校校舎に'''男子部'''、旧宮崎県女子師範学校校舎に'''女子部'''を設置。 **本科 （3年制。[[旧制中等教育学校|中等学校]]卒対象）・予科 （2年制。高等小学校卒対象） を設置。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年） **5月11日： 空襲で校舎倒壊。男子部予科生徒6名死亡。 **6月6日： 本科生、動員先の名古屋で7名爆死。 **8月12日： 宮崎大空襲で男子部・女子部ともに全焼。 **9月17日： [[枕崎台風]]で焼け残り寮舎倒壊。 **11月： 仮校舎で授業再開。男子部は[[川南町|川南村]]旧軍用施設、女子部は[[宮崎神宮]]徴古館を使用。 *[[1947年]]（昭和22年） **2月： [[宮崎大学教育学部附属小学校|附属小学校]]改築。 **4月： [[学制改革]]により、附属国民学校を附属小学校と[[宮崎大学教育学部附属中学校|附属中学校]]（[[新制中学校]]）に改組。 **9月： 川南から復帰。 *[[1949年]]（昭和24年）[[5月31日]]： 新制「'''宮崎大学'''」が発足。 **宮崎師範学校は[[宮崎青年師範学校]]と共に学芸学部の母体として包括された。在校生は「'''宮崎大学宮崎師範学校'''」の生徒となる。 *[[1951年]]（昭和26年）3月： 宮崎大学宮崎師範学校 （旧制）、廃止。
== 歴代校長 == {{節スタブ}}
;宮崎県師範学校（前身諸校を含む） *太田忠恕：1885年2月 - 1886年6月 *遠藤正：1886年6月 - 1908年5月1日 *[[東基吉]]：1908年5月1日<ref>『官報』第7452号、明治41年5月2日。</ref> - 10月14日<ref>『宮崎県大百科事典』（[[1983年]]） 878頁-879頁は、第3代校長のみ名前を挙げていない。</ref> *本田嘉種：1908年10月14日 - 1910年4月23日 *児玉鑑三：1910年4月23日 - 1915年4月27日<ref>『宮崎県大百科事典』（1983年） 878頁-879頁に挙げられている校長は第5代まで。</ref> *森岡格：1915年4月27日 -
;宮崎県女子師範学校 *山田恒治：1926年4月 - *井東豊彦：1930年9月 - *上地亀義 ：1937年3月 - ? <ref>『宮崎県大百科事典』（1983年） 879頁に挙げられている校長は第3代まで。</ref>
;官立宮崎師範学校 *長谷川亀太郎：1943年4月1日<ref>『官報』第4865号、昭和18年4月2日。</ref> - 1945年11月24日<ref name=kp5664>『官報』第5664号、昭和20年11月28日。</ref> *中村友平：1945年11月24日<ref name=kp5664/> - *片岡一亀:
== 校地の変遷と継承 == ;宮崎師範学校男子部 前身の宮崎県師範学校から引き継いだ現・宮崎市花殿町の校地を使用した。第二次世界大戦末期、1945年の空襲で校舎を焼失し、戦後の授業開始時は川南村 （現・川南町） の軍施設を使用したが、1947年9月に宮崎市に復帰した。旧男子部校地は、現在は[[宮崎大学教育学部附属中学校]]・[[宮崎大学教育学部附属小学校]]の校地となっている。
;宮崎師範学校女子部 前身の宮崎県女子師範学校から引き継いだ現・宮崎市船塚1丁目の校地を使用した。1945年の空襲で校舎を焼失し、戦後の授業開始時は宮崎神宮徴古館を借用した。旧女子部校地は後身の宮崎大学学芸学部 （[[1966年]]から教育学部） の校地として引き継がれ、同学部が[[1988年]]9月に現在の木花キャンパスに統合移転するまで使用された。旧女子部校地は、現在は[[宮崎公立大学]]の校地となっている。
== 著名な出身者 == {{See|宮崎大学の人物一覧#旧制学校時代の出身者}}
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 関連書籍 == *宮崎県（編） 『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 1』 宮崎県、[[2000年]]5月、260頁-263頁・656頁-665頁・945頁-953頁・1205頁-1208頁。 *宮崎県（編） 『宮崎県史 : 通史編 近・現代 2』 宮崎県、2000年5月、10頁-12頁。 *宮崎県（編） 『宮崎県史 : 別編 年表』 宮崎県、2000年5月。 *宮崎市史編纂委員会（編） 『宮崎市史』 宮崎市、[[1959年]]3月、632頁-634頁。 *宮崎市史編纂委員会（編） 『宮崎市史 : 続編（上）』 宮崎市、[[1978年]]3月、775頁-776頁。 *宮崎日日新聞社・宮崎県大百科事典刊行委員会（編） 『宮崎県大百科事典』 宮崎日日新聞社、[[1983年]]、「宮崎県師範学校」・「宮崎県女子師範学校」 の項。
== 関連項目 == *[[宮崎高等農林学校]]・[[宮崎青年師範学校]]・[[宮崎県工業専門学校 (旧制)]] - 新制[[宮崎大学]]の旧制前身校 *[[師範学校]] *[[旧制専門学校]] *[[学制改革]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ 宮崎大学教育学部] - 後身校 **[http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/faculty/history.html 沿革] *[http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ 木犀会] - 同窓会 *[https://www.the-miyanichi.co.jp/contents/index.php?itemid=2379&catid=74 宮崎師範学校予科生の慰霊碑建立] - 宮崎日日新聞 2007年10月8日付
{{日本の師範学校}} {{School-stub|pref=宮崎県}} {{デフォルトソート:みやさきしはん}} [[Category:日本の師範学校|みやさき]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧制教育機関|しはん]] [[Category:宮崎大学|旧しはん]] [[Category:宮崎市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Normal schools in Japan]] [[Category:Defunct schools in Japan]] [[Category:Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Miyazaki Normal School]] [[Category:New]] }}
[[sn:Miyazaki Normal School]] [[ja:宮崎師範学校]] [[en:Miyazaki Normal School]]

revision.text:
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|qq=draft}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学|qq=draft}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki City|ja|宮崎市|qq=draft}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|qq=draft}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県|qq=draft}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|qq=draft}} in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|qq=draft}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|ja|宮崎公立大学|qq=draft}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|ja|宮崎大学|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Educational reforms in Japan|ja|学制改革|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]
{{translated page|ja|宮崎師範学校|version=99990476|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Miyazaki Normal School]]

The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|qq=}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|qq=}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the [[:en:Miyazaki Municipal University|Miyazaki Municipal University]].

'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|qq=}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).

* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|qq=}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the [[:en:Miyazaki Municipal University|Miyazaki Municipal University]].

revision.comment: Bot: update category → University of Miyazaki
revision.text:
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|qq=}} established in [[Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[World War II]] in [[1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[World War II]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in [[:en:Miyazaki (city)|Miyazaki City]]).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|qq=}}. * [[1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] in [[1877]]).
* [[1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|qq=}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the [[:en:Miyazaki Municipal University|Miyazaki Municipal University]].
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|qq=}} * [[:en:Educational reform in occupied Japan|Educational reforms in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]
