revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454319]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} established in [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[:en:1884|1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in [[:en:Miyazaki (city)|Miyazaki City]]).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|1=Nomura Tsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125601}}. * [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[:en:1876|1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] in [[:en:1877|1877]]).
* [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[:en:1881|1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[:en:1884|1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[:en:1926|1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the [[:en:Miyazaki Municipal University|Miyazaki Municipal University]].
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * [[:en:Educational reform in occupied Japan|Educational reforms in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454319]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} established in [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[:en:1884|1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in [[:en:Miyazaki (city)|Miyazaki City]]).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|1=Nomura Tsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125601}}. * [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[:en:1876|1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] in [[:en:1877|1877]]).
* [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[:en:1881|1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[:en:1884|1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[:en:1926|1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the [[:en:Miyazaki Municipal University|Miyazaki Municipal University]].
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * [[:en:Educational reform in occupied Japan|Educational reforms in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

* [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[:en:1926|1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the [[:en:Miyazaki Municipal University|Miyazaki Municipal University]].
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * [[:en:Educational reform in occupied Japan|Educational reforms in Japan]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454319]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} established in [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]] during [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in [[:en:1884|1884]] (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * [[:en:1874|1874]] (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in [[:en:Miyazaki (city)|Miyazaki City]]).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|1=Nomura Tsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125601}}. * [[:en:1875|1875]] (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* [[:en:1876|1876]] (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]] in [[:en:1877|1877]]).
* [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * [[:en:1881|1881]] (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * [[:en:1884|1884]] (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * [[:en:1926|1926]] (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the [[:en:Miyazaki Municipal University|Miyazaki Municipal University]].
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:University of Miyazaki|University of Miyazaki]] * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * [[:en:Educational reform in occupied Japan|Educational reforms in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884}} (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|en|University of Miyazaki|WD=Q1071052|lt=University of Miyazaki}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * {{ill|1874|en|1874|WD=Q7761|lt=1874}} (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki (city)|en|Miyazaki (city)|WD=Q204308|lt=Miyazaki City}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|1=Nomura Tsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125601}}. * {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875}} (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* {{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876}} (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}} in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}}).
* {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}} (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881}} (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884}} (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|en|Miyazaki Municipal University|WD=Q3551804|lt=Miyazaki Municipal University}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|en|University of Miyazaki|WD=Q1071052|lt=University of Miyazaki}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * {{ill|Educational reform in occupied Japan|en|Educational reform in occupied Japan|WD=Q29657|lt=Educational reforms in Japan}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * {{ill|1874|en|1874|WD=Q7761|lt=1874}} (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki (city)|en|Miyazaki (city)|WD=Q204308|lt=Miyazaki City}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|1=Nomura Tsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125601}}. * {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875}} (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* {{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876}} (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}} in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877}}).
* {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}} (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881}} (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884}} (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926}} (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|en|Miyazaki Municipal University|WD=Q3551804|lt=Miyazaki Municipal University}}.

== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|en|University of Miyazaki|WD=Q1071052|lt=University of Miyazaki}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * {{ill|Educational reform in occupied Japan|en|Educational reform in occupied Japan|WD=Q29657|lt=Educational reforms in Japan}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]]
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} established in {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}} in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884}} (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|en|University of Miyazaki|WD=Q1071052|lt=University of Miyazaki}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * {{ill|1874|en|1874|WD=Q7761|lt=1874}} (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. * {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}} (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881}} (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===

* {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884}} (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>

* {{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}} (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}} in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}}).
* {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884|1=1884}} (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|en|Miyazaki Municipal University|WD=Q3551804|lt=Miyazaki Municipal University|1=Miyazaki Municipal University}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|en|University of Miyazaki|WD=Q1071052|lt=University of Miyazaki|1=University of Miyazaki}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * {{ill|Educational reform in occupied Japan|en|Educational reform in occupied Japan|WD=Q29657|lt=Educational reforms in Japan|1=Educational reform in occupied Japan}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮崎師範学校]]
'''Miyazaki Normal School''' (宮崎師範学校, Miyazaki Shihan Gakkō) was a {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} established in {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18).
This article also covers predecessor schools such as the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School''' and the '''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''.
== Overview ==
* It was established through the integration and nationalization of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School, and consisted of a men’s and women’s division. * Its origins date back to the establishment of the (prefectural) Miyazaki Normal School in {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884|1=1884}} (Meiji 17), one year after the establishment of modern Miyazaki Prefecture. * After {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, during the [[Educational reforms in Japan]], it became one of the predecessors of the Faculty of Arts and Sciences of the new {{ill|University of Miyazaki|en|University of Miyazaki|WD=Q1071052|lt=University of Miyazaki|1=University of Miyazaki}} (now the Faculty of Education). * Its alumni association is called the "'''Mokuseikai'''," a joint organization of both the old (pre-reform) and new (post-reform) faculties (Faculty of Arts and Sciences, Faculty of Education, and Faculty of Education and Culture).
== History ==
=== Early Prefectural Era ===
==== Temporary Elementary Lecture Center and Miyazaki School ====
* {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), October: The old Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Temporary Elementary Lecture Center''' (approximately one month of training).<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), pp. 260–263.</ref> * {{ill|1874|en|1874|WD=Q7761|lt=1874|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), August: Miyazaki Prefecture established the '''Miyazaki School''' (opened on September 2).<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 177.</ref> ** It aimed to provide secondary education and teacher training. It was located in the old Miyazaki Prefectural Temporary Government Office (current Tachibanadori 3-chome area in {{ill|Miyazaki (city)|en|Miyazaki (city)|WD=Q204308|lt=Miyazaki City|1=Miyazaki (city)}}).<ref>''Miyazaki City History'' (1959), p. 629. The city history was compiled approximately 50 years ago, so the current place name is uncertain.</ref> ** First principal: {{ill|Nomura Tsuna|ja|野村綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomura Tsuna|1=Nomura Tsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22125601}}. * {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} (Meiji 8): ** July: Revised educational regulations to admit middle school preparatory students aged 14 and above and normal students aged 17 and above. ** Winter: Miyazaki Prefecture invited 13 graduates of state-run normal schools, dispatching 10 to schools across the prefecture to teach.
=== Kagoshima Prefectural Era ===
==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* {{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}} (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}} in {{ill|1877|en|1877|WD=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}}).
* {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * {{ill|1884|en|1884|WD=Q7819|lt=1884|1=1884}} (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * {{ill|1926|en|1926|WD=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|en|Miyazaki Municipal University|WD=Q3551804|lt=Miyazaki Municipal University|1=Miyazaki Municipal University}}.
== References == <references/>
== Related Books == * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'', Miyazaki Prefecture, May 2000. * Miyazaki Prefecture (Ed.), ''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Chronological Table'', May 2000.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|University of Miyazaki|en|University of Miyazaki|WD=Q1071052|lt=University of Miyazaki|1=University of Miyazaki}} * {{ill|Normal school|ja|師範学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Normal school|1=normal schools in the Empire of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886109}} * {{ill|Educational reform in occupied Japan|en|Educational reform in occupied Japan|WD=Q29657|lt=Educational reforms in Japan|1=Educational reform in occupied Japan}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyazaki-u.ac.jp/edu/ University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education] * [http://www.miyazaki-mokuseikai.jp/ Mokuseikai Alumni Association]

==== First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center ====
* {{ill|1876|en|1876|qid=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}} (Meiji 9): ** August 21: The old Miyazaki Prefecture was absorbed into {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. ** November 2: The Miyazaki School was closed. ** November 4: The '''First District Elementary Normal Lecture Center''' was established (closed during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|qid=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}} in {{ill|1877|en|1877|qid=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}}).
* {{ill|1879|en|1879|qid=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}} (Meiji 12), April: Reopened as a cooperative school supported by 39 villages in Miyazaki District. * {{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), September: A tornado destroyed the school building, killing 16 elementary students. * {{ill|1885|en|1885|qid=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), May: The cooperative normal school was not reopened, as preparations were underway for the Miyazaki Normal School.
=== Prefectural Era ===
==== Original Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1883|en|1883|qid=Q7816|lt=1883|1=1883}} (Meiji 15): ** May 9: Modern Miyazaki Prefecture was established. ** September: The prefecture applied to establish a normal school.<ref>''History of Miyazaki Prefecture: Modern Period 1'' (2000), p. 657.</ref> * {{ill|1884|en|1884|qid=Q7819|lt=1884|1=1884}} (Meiji 17): ** September: Construction of the school building began. ** November 17: The establishment of the "Miyazaki Normal School" was approved.<ref>''Miyazaki Prefecture History: Chronological Table'' (2000), p. 185.</ref> * {{ill|1885|en|1885|qid=Q7821|lt=1885|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), February 28: The school building was completed, and the "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''" was opened.<ref>''Official Gazette'', March 17, 1885, Academic Affairs Section.</ref>
==== Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School ====
* {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), March: A women’s division was added to the Miyazaki Normal School. * {{ill|1926|en|1926|qid=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}} (Taishō 15), April 1: The women’s division was separated to form the "'''Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School'''" with dedicated facilities.
=== National Era ===
==== Miyazaki Normal School ====
* {{ill|1943|en|1943|qid=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), April 1: The Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School and Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School were merged to form the national "'''Miyazaki Normal School'''." ** A men’s division was established at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Normal School, and a women’s division at the former site of the Miyazaki Prefectural Women’s Normal School.
== Legacy ==
* The former men’s division campus now houses the {{ill|University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja|宮崎大学教育学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/University of Miyazaki Faculty of Education|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮崎大学|1=University of Miyazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1071052}}’s affiliated schools. * The former women’s division campus is now the {{ill|Miyazaki Municipal University|en|Miyazaki Municipal University|qid=Q3551804|lt=Miyazaki Municipal University|1=Miyazaki Municipal University}}.
